description: Per-item evaluation outputs for the LongevityBench-Mouse benchmark (Caltech Longevity Hackathon 2026, Track 01). Each line of every .jsonl file is one evaluated benchmark item. The frontend loads these and recomputes accuracy / Delta_recall / McNemar client-side — nothing is pre-baked.

record_schema.genotype_id: string — stable key; pairs the SAME item across the two conditions (and across runs).
record_schema.condition: geno_pheno = gene name SHOWN | pheno_only = gene name HIDDEN
record_schema.latency_s: number — seconds for the model call
record_schema.raw: string — the model's FULL response (reasoning trace + final 'Answer: X' line)

how_to_compute.accuracy: mean(correct) over items of a given condition
how_to_compute.delta_recall: accuracy(geno_pheno) - accuracy(pheno_only)  [chance ~= 0.50 on the balanced binary]
how_to_compute.mcnemar: pair items across conditions by genotype_id; b = correct-when-shown & wrong-when-hidden, c = the reverse; exact two-sided binomial p on min(b,c) vs Binom(b+c, 0.5)
how_to_compute.default_aggregation: 3-run majority vote: per (genotype_id, condition) majority-vote `correct` across the run files, THEN compute. Also support per-run and simple-mean modes.
how_to_compute.note: Majority-vote default lands near the authoritative headline numbers in results/stats.json; per-run and random-split modes will differ due to temp-0 non-determinism (~7.5% per-item flips). That is expected.

headline.note: Authoritative 3-run majority-voted numbers (CIs + McNemar p) for the STATIC sections. The explorer recomputes from the per-item files above and may differ slightly (temp-0 non-determinism).

ternary.note: LB-0154 is a 3-CLASS task (A=shortens, B=no-effect, C=extends), NOT the binary Delta_recall task. Do not run the binary accuracy/Delta_recall/McNemar logic on these. Compute overall accuracy + per-class recall + macro-F1 instead; the authoritative aggregate numbers are in headline.json -> ternary. The item drawer (gold / pred / correct / raw) works unchanged — A/B/C parse the same way.

single_gene.note: Epistasis-controlled rebuild of the controlled binary task with MULTI-GENE genotypes removed (single-gene only). Lives under web/data/single_gene/ with its own manifest + headline once the live run finishes. Compare its Delta_recall against the (mixed) controlled numbers above.
